steps.title: Simplify the equation
steps.content: Start with the absolute-value equation and divide both sides by $-2$: $$|x-7|=4x$$ Since an absolute value is never negative, $4x\ge 0$, so $x\ge 0$.

steps.title: Solve the valid branch
steps.content: The solution must be less than $7$, so $|x-7|=7-x$. Then $$7-x=4x$$ $$x=\frac{7}{5}$$ The negative value in choices A and C fails the $x\ge 0$ check, and choice D includes an extra value. Choice B is correct.
